title: Chapter 4 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Object-Oriented Dart!

questions.question: What is a class?
options: A function that returns a value
questions.explanation: A class is a blueprint from which objects are created.

questions.question: An object is an instance of a class.
questions.explanation: True, objects are concrete instances of classes.

questions.question: The keyword _____ is used to create a subclass.
questions.explanation: extends creates a subclass relationship.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about constructors?
options: They must have the same name as the class
They are called automatically when an object is created
questions.explanation: Constructors have the class name and are called upon object creation.

questions.question: Abstract classes can be instantiated.
questions.explanation: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated directly.

questions.question: What is a mixin used for?
options: To reuse code across unrelated classes
questions.explanation: Mixins allow code reuse without inheritance.

questions.question: To apply a mixin, use the _____ keyword.
questions.explanation: with is used to include mixins.

questions.question: A getter is defined using the 'get' keyword.
questions.explanation: Yes, getters are defined with 'get'.

questions.question: What does the '@override' annotation do?
options: It indicates a method overrides a superclass method
questions.explanation: @override marks that a method is intended to override a superclass method.

questions.question: An enum is declared using the _____ keyword.
questions.explanation: enum declares an enumerated type.

questions.question: Extensions can add new methods to existing classes.
questions.explanation: Extensions allow adding functionality to any type.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid OOP principle?
questions.explanation: Compilation is not an OOP principle.

questions.question: The _____ keyword is used to call the superclass constructor.
questions.explanation: super refers to the superclass.

questions.question: All classes in Dart inherit from Object.
questions.explanation: Yes, Object is the root of the class hierarchy.

questions.question: What is the output of: `print(3 is int);`
questions.explanation: 3 is an int, so 'is' returns true.

questions.question: A method without a body in an abstract class is called an _____ method.
questions.explanation: Abstract methods have no implementation.

questions.question: You can have multiple constructors in a Dart class.
questions.explanation: Yes, using named constructors.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to refer to the current instance?
questions.explanation: this refers to the current object.

questions.question: A _____ is a special method that is called when an object is created.
questions.explanation: Constructors initialize new objects.

questions.question: Private members in Dart are denoted by an underscore (_).
questions.explanation: Yes, identifiers starting with _ are library-private.

questions.question: What is polymorphism?
options: The ability of a variable to take multiple forms
The bundling of data and methods
questions.explanation: Polymorphism allows objects to be treated as instances of their parent class.

questions.question: To make a class implement another class's interface, use the _____ keyword.
questions.explanation: implements forces a class to provide implementations of all methods.

questions.question: A class can extend multiple classes in Dart.
questions.explanation: Dart supports single inheritance only.

questions.question: What does the `covariant` keyword do?
options: It allows overriding a method with a more specific parameter type
It marks a method as asynchronous
It indicates a class is abstract
questions.explanation: covariant relaxes type checks in overrides.

questions.question: A _____ is a compile-time constant that can be used in switch statements.
questions.explanation: Enums are ideal for switch cases because they have a fixed set of values.
